summary: Diff implements frontend-side improvements for registration/navigation and error handling, and shows partial backend changes to return #failure for some adminRegister cases. However, the backend changes are truncated and do not provide evidence that driverRegister/travellerRegister (and the full adminRegister logic) were updated to avoid Runtime.trap in expected error cases, nor that getCallerUserProfile/UserProfile.role encoding was aligned end-to-end.

missing_requirements.requirement: Update backend registration methods (adminRegister/driverRegister/travellerRegister) to avoid using Runtime.trap for expected registration errors; return RegistrationStatus.failure with an English message instead.
missing_requirements.reason: Only a portion of adminRegister is shown returning #failure; there is no diff evidence for driverRegister/travellerRegister behavior, and the backend file is truncated so it cannot be confirmed that traps were removed across all expected error paths.
evidence: backend/main.mo (truncated; shows requireRegisteredUser still using Runtime.trap and only partial adminRegister logic)

missing_requirements.requirement: Fix backend role/profile values so the frontend can reliably determine admin/driver/traveller based on getCallerUserProfile output, aligning backend UserProfile.role encoding with generated frontend types.
missing_requirements.reason: Frontend routing now compares against UserRole.admin and uses busNumber presence, but the diff does not include getCallerUserProfile implementation or evidence that backend UserProfile.role values returned match the candid-generated frontend UserRole type across roles.
evidence: backend/main.mo (truncated; type definitions shown but not getCallerUserProfile implementation/returned values)
frontend/src/App.tsx (role routing logic updated, but depends on backend correctness)

missing_requirements.requirement: Enable workable admin registration: allow first admin if none exists; otherwise restrict and return friendly failure messages without traps.
missing_requirements.reason: The diff shows an isAdminRegistered() helper and some #failure messages for unauthorized admin creation, but the adminRegister logic is truncated, so it cannot be confirmed that 'first admin can be created when none exists' is fully implemented end-to-end and that no traps remain in admin registration paths.
evidence: backend/main.mo (truncated; shows isAdminRegistered() and partial adminRegister)
